title: Psacalium paucicapitatum
taxonomy: Psacalium paucicapitatum belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is part of the genus Psacalium. Further classification details may include its specific rank within the plant kingdom.

intro: Psacalium paucicapitatum, also known as few-headed psacalium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Psacalium paucicapitatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Psacalium paucicapitatum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems are used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
